metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5456
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 6: microtubule plus-end tracking protein CLASP2 regulates spontaneous synaptic transmission.

Whole-cell recordings of overexpressed human CLASP2 and shRNA knockdowns in mouse hippocampal neuronal cultures showed an increase in mEPSC frequency, suggesting that CLASP2 alters excitatory presynaptic neurotransmitter release machinery. A small but significant mEPSC amplitude effect suggests CLASP2 overexpression affects postsynaptic receptors as well.

Figure 7: CLASP2 affects synaptic morphology and regulates synaptic protein levels.

Electron microscopy of control and human CLASP2 overexpression in mouse hippocampal neuronal cultures showed "a significant 28% increase in presynaptic terminal circumference in neurons overexpressing human CLASP2, which may be a consequence of increased vesicle fusion with the plasma membrane and could be a cause for the functional effect in spontaneous synaptic transmission".

Immunofluorescence labeling of pre- and post-synaptic marker proteins showed an increase of synapses upon CLASP2 overexpression, and a decrease on shRNA knockdown. Further, quantitative immunoblotting showed that "CLASP2 specifically regulates synaptic proteins, possibly through stabilization of the cytoskeleton to directly affect synaptic activity". Biotinylation assays showed that GluA1 receptor subunit expression at the cell surface was also increased in CLASP2 overexpressing neurons.

authors summarise;
"In summary, our results suggest that the control of microtubule organization by CLASP2 may represent a mechanism for regulated growth cone motility and synaptic growth and plasticity. We find that increased CLASP2 expression leads to accelerated and enhanced neuron and synaptic development.". Experimental description: plasmids:
"Full-length human CLASP2 (National Center for Biotechnology Information reference sequence NM_015097.2) was PCR amplified from a full-length cDNA clone (ImaGenes) and subcloned into pEGFP-C1 (Clontech) to create an N-terminal enhanced green fluorescence protein (EGFP) tag. EGFP-CLASP2 and EGFP control were then subcloned into the lentiviral vector pFUW and sequence verified. The shRNA constructs for mouse CLASP2 include GCATCAGTCCTTTCAACAAGT and GAACTTGAAGAGACGTTAAAT, and control shRNA CCGCAGGTATGCACGCGT are all cloned into the pLKO.1 vector (Addgene plasmid 10879)."
